complainant_information.goods_or_services_description: On 2025-09-15, the check writer, Michael Alan Porter, came into Lincoln Hardware & Home Supply, LLC and purchased a combination of construction materials and tools for what he described as a small remodel and repair job on a rental property off Old Highway 51. The total included: (1) 18 sheets of 1/2" exterior-grade plywood; (2) 12 treated 2x6x10 boards; (3) 5 gallons of exterior white latex paint (Lincoln brand); (4) 2 gallons of primer; (5) a mid-range corded circular saw (DeWalt brand); (6) 2 heavy-duty orange extension cords (50 ft each); (7) a box of 3" exterior deck screws (5 lbs); (8) assorted paint rollers, brushes, painter's tape, and drop cloths; and (9) a basic safety kit including goggles, dust masks, and work gloves. The items were all new, taken directly from our retail inventory, and scanned at the register under our standard pricing. Mr. Porter loaded the lumber and paint into the bed of his white Ford F-150 pickup truck with our assistance. The check he wrote was to cover the full purchase price including tax; no discounts other than a standard in-store special on paint were applied. The goods left our premises with him immediately after the transaction and have not been returned. We have a detailed receipt on file under invoice #28419 and matching security camera footage from the front checkout counter and parking lot showing the transaction, the loading of the materials, and Mr. Porter driving away with the merchandise in his truck.
complainant_information.notification_of_improper_services_or_def: We have never been contacted by Mr. Porter, or by anyone on his behalf, claiming that the products he purchased were defective, damaged, or unsuitable for the job he described. There have been no phone calls, emails, or in-person visits to request a refund, exchange, or store credit related to this transaction. Our store email account (info@lincolnhardwarems.com) receives general customer service inquiries, and we have searched by his name "Porter" and the transaction date and found no correspondence. Our cashier notes and incident logs for the week of 2025-09-15 through 2025-09-22 show no mention of any problem with these goods. In addition, our store policy is that any significant complaint (especially involving power tools or large quantities of building materials) is documented in our daily manager's log; a review of that log shows no entries related to this customer or this sale. In short, we have received no notice whatsoever that the services or goods involved in this check transaction were improper, defective, or disputed in any way.

check_writer_information.additional_information_location_of_check: The address listed above is the same one that appears on Mr. Porter's printed checks and matched the address on the Mississippi driver's license he presented at the time of purchase. When our cashier, Daniel Holloway, verified his ID, he noted that Mr. Porter mentioned he has lived in the Brookhaven area "most of his life" and works as a self-employed handyman and small contractor, often doing repair work on rental houses around town. Mr. Porter stated that he banks with Magnolia Community Bank on Brookway Boulevard and that his checking account was a "business account" under his own name. He arrived at the store driving a white Ford F-150 pickup truck, approximately model year 2014–2018, with some visible rust along the rear wheel wells and a ladder rack in the bed. The truck had Mississippi license plates starting with the letters "LBK" (Daniel is not certain of the exact numbers following). Mr. Porter also mentioned during casual conversation that he has two school-age children enrolled in the Lincoln County School District and that he was trying to finish this particular job before the weekend so he could "take the kids to the game." These personal details, along with his local phone number and the fact that his address is within about 10–15 minutes of our store, may assist in locating him.
